  "findings": [
    "Three small circular bumps in paint film along one stud line, consistent with seasonal lumber shrinkage."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "No corrective action required. Monitor during remaining warranty period."
  ],
  "rationale": "Screw pops due to lumber shrinkage are a common cosmetic occurrence in drywall, not a structural or code defect. Original fastener spacing met schedule, and no cracking, movement, or moisture staining is present. Per GA-216, minor pops are acceptable and do not require repair unless they compromise finish integrity, which is not the case here.",
  "references": [
    "GA-216 Application and Finishing of Gypsum Panel Products"
  ]
}
